cases.desc: dispatch(source_type='pdf', ...) returns RawDocument with correct id/source_type/text/timestamp types
dispatch(source_type='email', ...) returns RawDocument with correct shape
dispatch(source_type='ticket', data=...) returns RawDocument with correct shape
dispatch(source_type='ticket', row=...) returns RawDocument with correct shape
PDF RawDocument.structured_fields has correct page_count
Email RawDocument.structured_fields has sender/subject/thread; text == body
Ticket RawDocument.structured_fields has ticket_id/status/priority/etc + comment_count; text == rendered blob
All three produce a RawDocument with identical field NAMES (id, source_type, text, structured_fields, timestamp) regardless of source type
dispatch('pdf', ...) actually calls normalize_pdf, not another normalizer (monkeypatch spy)
dispatch('email', ...) actually calls normalize_email
dispatch('ticket', data=...) actually calls normalize_ticket_json, not csv_row
dispatch('ticket', row=...) actually calls normalize_ticket_csv_row, not json
dispatch() with an unrecognized source_type raises ValueError
dispatch('ticket', ...) with neither data nor row raises ValueError
dispatch('ticket', ...) with both data and row raises ValueError (ambiguous)
dispatch('pdf', ...) without path raises ValueError
dispatch_ticket_json and dispatch_ticket_csv_row on the same logical ticket (sample_1 json vs csv) produce identical text and structured_fields
caller-supplied timestamp is used verbatim instead of now()
omitted timestamp defaults to a timezone-aware UTC datetime close to call time
caller-supplied doc_id is passed through verbatim as RawDocument.id for all source types
